title: Types of profit
description: Practice what you have learned about economic and accounting profit in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
Malinalli is a painter and coder who likes both professions equally. She can earn \$80,000 as a painter or \$70,000 per year as a coder, and has no other costs associated with either profession.

**What is Malinalli’s economic profit if she chooses to be a coder instead of a painter?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Economic profit is the difference between money taken in and all costs, both explicit and implicit.
For a person, the money taken in is their income.

Opportunity costs are the cost of the next best alternative, and are implicit costs.
If Mallinalli chooses to be a painter her next best alternative is being a coder. Therefore, her opportunity cost of being a painter is the income she would have earned as a coder.

Mallinalli’s economic profit equals $-\$10,000$:

$\begin{align}\text{Economic profit} & = \text{Total revenue} -\text{explicit costs} - \text{implicit costs}\\\\
& =\$70,000 - \$80,000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes what a firm’s objective is?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following explains why economic profit is always less than accounting profit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Economic profit includes more costs than accounting profit.
Economic profit includes more revenues than accounting profit.
Economic profit includes explicit costs but not implicit costs, and accounting profit includes both of these.
Economic profit includes less revenues than accounting profit
Economic profit includes more utility than accounting profit.

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a firm is earning positive economic profits, which of the following must also be true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Its total revenue is greater than explicit costs but not implicit and explicit costs.
Its total revenue is less than explicit costs and implicit costs.
Its explicit costs are greater than its implicit costs.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following ensures that a firm is earning an economic profit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The price of the good is higher than the firm’s average total cost.
Total revenue is higher than explicit costs
Average total economic cost is minimized
The firm earns an accounting profit.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What must be true if a firm is earning only a normal profit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**All of the following are included in the calculation of accounting profit EXCEPT which of option?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Leatherbound, Inc. produces and sells baseballs in a competitive market. The total cost of producing $200$ baseballs is $\$900$. The price of a baseball is $\$10$. 

**What is this firm’s economic profit (or loss)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Economic profit is the difference between a firm’s total revenue and its total cost:

$\text{Economic profit} = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total costs}$

Total revenue is the price of a good times the quantity of goods sold:

$\text{Total revenue} = \$10 \times 200$
Total revenue is \$2000:

$\begin{align} \text{Total revenue} & = \$10 \times 200\\\\
&=\$2000\end{align}$
This firm’s profit is $\$1100$:

$\begin{align}\text{Economic profit} & = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total costs}\\\\
& =(\$10 \times 200) - \$900\\\\
& =\$2000 - \$900\\\\
& =\$1100\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Nakawé, LLC  produces and sells greeting cards in a competitive market. The total cost of producing $1000$ greeting cards is $\$4000$. The price of a greeting card is $\$4$. 

**What is this firm’s economic profit (or loss)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Economic profit is the difference between a firm’s total revenue and its total cost:

$\text{Economic profit} = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total costs}$

Total revenue is the price of a good times the quantity of goods sold:

$\text{Total revenue} = \$4 \times 1000$
Total revenue is \$4000:

$\begin{align}\text{Total revenue} & = \$4 \times 1000\\\\
&=\$4000\end{align}$
This firm earns no economic profit:

$\begin{align}\text{Economic profit} & = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total costs}\\\\
& =(\$4 \times 1000) - \$4000\\\\
& =\$4000 - \$4000\\\\
&=\$0 \end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
T-Rex Enterprises produces and sells dinosaur costumes in a competitive market. The total cost of producing $100$ dinosaur costumes is $\$9000$. The price of a dinosaur costume is $\$80$. 

**What is this firm’s economic profit (or loss)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Economic profit is the difference between a firm’s total revenue and its total cost:

$\text{Economic profit} = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total costs}$

Total revenue is the price of a good times the quantity of goods sold:

$\text{Total revenue} = \$80 \times 100$

This firm’s has a loss of $\$1000$:

$\begin{align} \text{Economic profit} & = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total costs}\\\\
& =(\$80 \times 1000) - \$9000\\\\
& =\$8000 - \$9000\\\\
& =-\$1000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A firm produces $200$ pies and sells each pie for $\$20$. The explicit cost of making the pies is $\$2000$. Additionally, the opportunity cost of the firm’s owner is $\$1000$, and the building the firm is in could be rented to someone else for $\$700$. 

**What is this firm’s economic profit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Economic profits are total revenue minus total economic costs, which includes all explicit and implicit costs:

$\begin{align} \text{Economic Profit}& = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total cost}\\\\
& =\text{Total revenue} - (\text{Explicit cost} + \text{Implicit cost})\end{align}$

Total revenue is price times quantity:

$\text{Total revenue} = \text{Price} \times \text{Quantity}$

Total revenue is $\$4000$:

$\begin{align} \text{Total revenue} &= \text{Price} \times \text{Quantity}\\\\
& =\$20 \times 200\\\\
& =\$4000 \end{align}$
Explicit costs are $\$2000$, and implicit costs, in this case, are the opportunity cost of the entrepreneurs’ time and the opportunity cost of capital:

$\text{Total cost} = \$2000 + \$1000 + \$700$
Economic profit is $\$300$:

$\begin{align} \text{Economic Profit} & = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total cost}\\\\
& = \$4000 - (\$2000 + \$1000 +\$500)\\\\
& =\$4000 - \$3500\\\\
& =\$300\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Yamanik Unlimited produces and sells $10{,}000$ greeting cards in a competitive market. It has explicit costs of $\$4{,}000$ and opportunity cost of capital of $\$1{,}000$.  If the owner of Yamanik Unlimited closed her business for good and took another job, she could be earning $\$31{,}000$ per year. The price of a greeting card is $\$5$.

**What is Yamanik Unlimited’s economic profit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Economic profits are total revenue minus total economic costs, which includes all explicit and implicit costs:

$\begin{align} \text{Economic Profit} & = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total cost}\\\\
& =\text{Total revenue} - (\text{Explicit cost} + \text{Implicit cost})\end{align}$
Total revenue is price times quantity.

Total revenue is $\$50,000$:

$\begin{align} \text{Total revenue} &= \text{Price} \times \text{Quantity}\\\\
& =\$5 \times 10{,}000\\\\
& =\$50{,}000\end{align}$

Explicit costs are $\$4{,}000$, and implicit costs, in this case, are the opportunity cost of the entrepreneurs time and the opportunity cost of capital:

$\text{Total cost} = \$4000 + \$1000 + \$31{,}000$
Economic profit is $\$14{,}000$:

$\begin{align}\text{Economic Profit} & = \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total cost}\\\\
&= \$50{,}000 - (\$4{,}000 + \$1{,}000 +\$31{,}000)\\\\
& =\$50{,}000 - \$36{,}000\\\\
& =\$14{,}000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
In $2018$, Arctic Enterprises produced and sold $500$ sleds in a competitive market at a price of $\$100$ each. The cost of materials and labor was $\$7{,}000$. The opportunity cost of capital was $\$10{,}000$ and the opportunity cost of the entrepreneur’s time was $\$40{,}000$.

**What is Arctic Enterprises’ economic profit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Economic profits are total revenue minus total economic costs, which includes all explicit and implicit costs:

$\begin{align} \text{Economic Profit} &= \text{Total revenue} -\text{Total cost}\\\\
& =\text{Total revenue} - (\text{Explicit cost} + \text{Implicit cost})\end{align}$
Explicit costs are $\$7{,}000$, and implicit costs, in this case, are the opportunity cost of the entrepreneurs’  time and the opportunity cost of capital:

$\text{Total cost} = \$7{,}000 + \$10{,}000 + \$40{,}000$

This firm is making an economic loss of $-\$7{,}000$:

$\begin{align} \text{Economic Profit} &= \text{Total revenue} - \text{Total cost}\\\\
&= \$50{,}000 - (\$7{,}000 + \$10{,}000 + \$40{,}000)\\\\
& = \$50{,}000 - \$57{,}000\\\\
& =-\$7{,}000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
When Yooko Industries makes $2000$ widgets, the cost of producing a typical widget is $\$5$, which includes implicit costs of $\$2$. 

**What is Yooko Industries economic profit (or loss) and accounting profit (or loss) if the price of a widget is $\$4$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To calculate economic profit using price and average total cost, use this formula:

$\text{Economic profit} = (\text{Price} -\text{Average total cost}) \times \text{Quantity}$
Average total cost is the total cost of producing a typical widget.
Given a price of $\$4$, the average total cost of $\$5$, and a quantity of $2000$, Yooko Industries’ has economic losses of $\$2000$:

$\begin{align} \text{Economic profit} & = (\$4 - \$5) \times 2000\\\\
& =(-\$1) \times 2000\\\\
&=-\$2000\end{align}$
To find accounting profits, you must remove the implicit cost of each unit from the total cost of each unit to find the explicit cost of each unit:

$\begin{align} \text{Total cost} & = \text{Explicit cost} + \text{implicit cost}\\\\
\text{Total cost} -\text{implicit cost} & = \text{Explicit cost}\end{align}$
The explicit cost of each unit is $\$3$:

$\begin{align} \text{Total cost} -\text{implicit cost} & = \text{Explicit cost}\\\\
\$5-\$2 &= \$3\end{align}$

This firm’s accounting profit is $\$2000$:

$\begin{align} \text{Accounting profit} &= (\text{Price} - \text{Average explicit cost}) \times \text{Quantity}\\\\
&=(\$4 - \$3) \times 2000\\\\
& =\$1 \times 2000\\\\
& =\$2000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Michonne’s Mystery Meats, LLC  produces $100$ pounds of meat each day and sells it at a price of $\$9$. The average total cost of producing each pound of meat is $\$6$.

**What is this firm’s economic profits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To calculate economic profit using price and average total cost, use this formula:

$\text{Economic profit} = (\text{Price} -\text{Average total cost)} \times \text{Quantity}$

Given a price of $\$9$, an average total cost of $\$6$, and a quantity of $100$, this firm has economic profits of $\$300$.

$\begin{align} \text{Economic profit} & = (\$9 - \$6) \times 100\\\\
&=(\$3) \times 100\\\\
&=\$300\end{align}$
